003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
024
025/**
026 * Subclass of {@link SerializerSession} for stream-based serializers.
027 *
028 * <h5 class='topic'>Description</h5>
029 *
030 * This class is the parent class of all byte-based serializers.
031 * <br>It has 1 abstract method to implement...
032 * <ul>
033 *    <li>{@link #doSerialize(SerializerPipe, Object)}
034 * </ul>
035 *
036 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
037 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
038 * </ul>
039 *
040 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
041 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SerializersAndParsers">Serializers and Parsers</a>
042 * </ul>
043 */
044@SuppressWarnings({
045   "java:S110", // Deep inheritance inherent to the serializer/parser session hierarchy
046   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
047   "resource"   // Internal helpers return Closeables wired into pipe lifecycle; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
048})
049public class OutputStreamSerializerSession extends SerializerSession {
050
051   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
052   private static final String ARG_ctx = "ctx";
053
054   /**
055    * Builder class.
056    */
057   @SuppressWarnings({
058      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
059   })
060   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends SerializerSession.Builder<SELF> {
061
062      private OutputStreamSerializer ctx;
063
064      /**
065       * Constructor
066       *
067       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
068       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
069       */
070      protected Builder(OutputStreamSerializer ctx) {
071         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
072         this.ctx = ctx;
073      }
074
075      @Override
076      public OutputStreamSerializerSession build() {
077         return new OutputStreamSerializerSession(this);
078      }
079
080   }
081
082   /**
083    * Concrete default builder leaf for the non-subclassed {@code create()} path (CRTP terminal).
084    */
085   public static final class DefaultBuilder extends Builder<DefaultBuilder> {
086
087      DefaultBuilder(OutputStreamSerializer ctx) {
088         super(ctx);
089      }
090   }
091
092   /**
093    * Creates a new builder for this object.
094    *
095    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
096    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
097    * @return A new builder.
098    */
099   @SuppressWarnings({
100      "java:S1452" // Builder<?> wildcard return intentional; callers use it to construct session instances polymorphically
101   })
102   public static Builder<?> create(OutputStreamSerializer ctx) {
103      return new DefaultBuilder(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
104   }
105
106   private final OutputStreamSerializer ctx;
107
108   /**
109    * Constructor.
110    *
111    * @param builder The builder for this object.
112    */
113   protected OutputStreamSerializerSession(Builder<?> builder) {
114      super(builder);
115      ctx = builder.ctx;
116   }
117
118   @Override /* Overridden from SerializerSession */
119   public final boolean isWriterSerializer() { return false; }
120
121   /**
122    * Convenience method for serializing an object to a <code><jk>byte</jk></code>.
123    *
124    * @param o The object to serialize.
125    * @return The output serialized to a byte array.
126    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
127    */
128   @Override /* Overridden from SerializerSession */
129   public final byte[] serialize(Object o) throws SerializeException {
130      var baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
131      try {
132         serialize(o, baos);
133         baos.flush();
134      } catch (IOException e) {
135         throw new SerializeException(e); // Should never happen.
136      }
137      return baos.toByteArray();
138   }
139
140   @Override /* Overridden from SerializerSession */
141   public final String serializeToString(Object o) throws SerializeException {
142      // Stream-to-string is a debug / display convenience for binary serializer output (BSON / MsgPack /
143      // CBOR / Prototext / Parquet).  It always emits HEX irrespective of the configured BinaryFormat so that
144      // downstream tooling (test fixtures, log lines, REPL output) gets a stable, copy-pasteable
145      // representation of the binary frame regardless of how the surrounding context configures byte[]
146      // cell encoding.
147      return BinaryFormat.HEX.format(serialize(o));
148   }
149
150   @Override /* Overridden from SerializerSession */
151   protected SerializerPipe createPipe(Object output) {
152      return new SerializerPipe(output);
153   }
154
155   /**
156    * Binary output format.
157    *
158    * @see MarshallingContext.Builder#binaryFormat(BinaryFormat)
159    * @return
160    *    The format to use for the {@link #serializeToString(Object)} method on stream-based serializers when converting byte arrays to strings.
161    */
162   protected final BinaryFormat getBinaryFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getBinaryFormat(); }
163
164   /**
165    * Returns whether this binary serializer has a native byte-array wire type.
166    *
167    * <p>
168    * When {@code true}, {@code byte[]} payloads bypass the configured
169    * {@link BinaryFormat} dispatch in {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.swaps.BinarySwap} and the variant
170    * binary swap installed by {@code MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor}, and are written as raw
171    * bytes through the serializer's native binary opcode (e.g. MsgPack {@code bin} family, CBOR
172    * byte-string major type 2, BSON binary subtype {@code 0x05}).  When {@code false}, the
173    * configured {@link BinaryFormat} fires for non-{@code NOT_SET} formats and produces a text
174    * wire form (HEX / BASE64 / etc.) that the serializer emits as a string in its native string
175    * column / literal type — used by serializers whose wire format has no native byte-array
176    * primitive (Parquet column writer, binary RDF formats).
177    *
178    * <p>
179    * Defaults to {@code true}.  Subclasses without a native byte-array wire type
180    * (e.g. {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parquet.ParquetSerializerSession} and the binary RDF
181    * sessions) override and return {@code false}.
182    *
183    * @return {@code true} if this serializer has a native byte-array wire type.
184    */
185   public boolean hasNativeBytes() {
186      return true;
187   }
188}
